Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-17-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Zarich appearing for Lenore Gerard, Guardian ad Litem. Andrew Schultz for and with (temporary trustee) Bennett Hong. Audrey Shields & Tenesha Jackson for Steven Payne. William Taylor also present.

Mr. Schultz stated that there is a threshold issue, that a bond is required and cannot be waived by a trust beneficiary, that there are properties in San Francisco and Oakland, that the Oakland property consists of multiple buildings on the same lot, that the insurance has been cancelled, there are empty units, that there is quite a bit of debt, that there is $150,000.00 in outstanding taxes and interest is accruing, that there are liens on the Oakland property, that fees are due for 3 attorneys and 2 fiduciaries totaling $300,000.00, that as a non resident Steven Payne must post bond, that Mr. Payne is a resident of Texas but wants to move here and that bond should be in the amount of $300,000.00. Ms. Jackson cited 14.39 & 14.38 stating that bond is not required as Mr. Payne is the sole beneficiary, that the majority of the trust is real property, that a minimum bond amount of $20,000.00 is sufficient, that the rest of the funds can be in blocked accounts and that her client would like to do away with the professional fiduciaries to reduce costs. Ms. Zarich stated that she is in agreement with Mr. Schultz. Mr. Taylor indicated that he has nothing to add. Mr. Schultz stated that they will cooperate if Mr. Payne is appointed, that in 2011 Mr. Payne sought conservatorship and was objected to, that the trustee provisions track the original trust which is under court supervision, that Mr. Payne was objected to as a fiduciary, that Mr. Payne is named as a beneficiary but not as a trustee, that there are unpaid taxes and debts, that the property is problematic, that this petition has been pending since December and had it been decided on they would almost be finished and not incurring all of the expenses. Ms. Jackson stated that the clear intent was for Mr. Payne to inherit from the trust and that if they continue down this path there will be nothing left. The petition to appoint a successor trustee and the petition for appointment of permanent successor trustee was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar May-27-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Taylor for (former trustee) William Diggs. Jennifer Zarich appearing for Lenore Gerard, Guardian ad Litem. Andrew Schultz for and with (conservator) Shirley Shaw, and for (temporary trustee) Bennett Hong.

Mr. Schultz stated that there was opposition on this matter and was expecting Ms. Shields to be in attendance. The Court stated that Steven Payne has appeared in the past to object, that Mr. Hong has been appointed and taken over as Trustee and asked if anyone had heard from Mr. Payne or his counsel. Mr. Schultz stated that he spoke with Ms. Shields last week regarding loans, sent an email to her on Friday but has not heard anything since. The Court stated that the burden was for the trustee to consider selling the property, for Mr. Payne to come up with a realistic plan to avoid the sale and to communicate with everyone and present it to the court and asked if anyone has heard of another plan. Mr. Schultz reported that he had spoken with Ms. Shields, that they wished to pursue a loan, that they needed information/approval from the trustee but with new costs, new liabilities the trustee does not see a way that a loan is feasible and that this was communicated to Ms. Shields with no response. The petition to withdraw funds from blocked account and petition to sell rental real property, etc. was granted (subject to receipt & review of the order). Mr. Schultz is to prepare a proposed order.

Petition To Withdraw Funds From Blocked Account To Pay Trust Expenses And Petition To Sell Rental Real Property In Order To Pay Taxes
Off Calendar Feb-17-2015 Continued To Apr-21-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Taylor for (trustee) William Diggs & (temporary trustee) Herb Thomas. Jennifer Zarich appearing for Lenore Gerard, Guardian ad Litem for Mr. Leslie Payne. Andrew Schultz for Shirley Shaw (conservator of the person of the settlor). Audrey Shields and Tenesha Jackson for (son) Steven Payne.

The court stated that there was an objection to the petition to sell real property by Mr. Payne. Ms. Zarich stated that the guardian ad litem had no objection to the sale of property. Mr. Schultz provided background information on Mr. Leslie Payne stating that Mr. Diggs had been caring for him, he is in need of a high level of care, needs twice daily suctioning for swallowing issues, there is a goal to move him back to San Francisco to a skilled nursing facility (SNF), that there is a new physician evaluating him but is not sure of the prognosis at this time. Ms. Shields reported that Mr. Leslie Payne has been in hospice for a long period of time, is not currently on life support, spends much of the day sleeping, that his son is unable to obtain information and that they will be filing a petition for appointment of Steven Payne as conservator of the person. Ms. Shields also reported that the property in Oakland is inhabited by Section 8 tenants, that the Housing Authority has not reported any problems and requests a 60-90 day continuance to find out what is going on. The court noted that there are issues of a substantial tax bill as well as substantial repairs needed on the property. Ms. Jackson stated that a loan could be taken out against the properties, it would leave the property in the estate, the loan payments could be paid with the loan and that the heirs would inherit the loan with the properties. Mr. Taylor reported that without a sale they will not be able to meet the tax obligations as well as substantial utility bills and property taxes, that the Oakland property is substantially deteriorated, that they have looked at loans (as indicated in the declaration of Herb Thomas) but stated that waiting would not cause additional problems. The petition to withdraw funds from blocked account and petition to sell rental property was continued to April 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to provide time for the temporary trustee to research alternatives and for the objectors to come up with a plan and assist.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Reimbursement For Trustee And Fees For Petitioner'S Attorney.
Off Calendar Dec-28-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Taylor for William Diggs (Trustee). Lenore Gerard Court appointed Guardian Ad Litem for Lelsie Payne.

Mr. Taylor stated that Mr. Freedman waived notice and does not intend to make an appearance. Mr. Taylor will prepare a proposed order based upon consents of the Parties allowing the First Account so that they can have starting figures for the Second Account. The Second Account will go from December 21, 2011 through December 31, 2012. Status on filing of the Second Account was set for March 26, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Taylor stated that Mr. Diggs does not qualify for a bond beyond $100,000.00, referred to the personal budget (exhibit F) and the income and expenses (page 3 of the petition), and stated that the $3,900.00 per month amount was an agreed Trustee fee in 2001 and that he did not know how they came to that figure. Ms. Gerard stated that Mr. Diggs provides care for Mr. Payne as well and that care is included in that amount, that the $4,800.00 is for the Care Attendants and that Mr. Freedman was not objecting to the petition. Mr. Taylor stated that he is requesting an amount of $1,490.00 for legal fees, referred to the billing schedule/time schedule, stated that the $56,000.00 amount was a historical amount that has already been paid, that it would be transferred to the Trust and spoke of the Court allowing monthly withdrawals in the amount of $7,000.00. The Court stated that $56,000.00 should be reimbursed and that $7,000.00 be paid for the months of January, February and March. The Court granted the Petition for Reimbursement for Trustee and Fees for Petitioner's Attorney, and the Petition to Withdraw Funds Form the Blocked Acccount (subject to review of the orders).
